description: Initialize Node.js/TypeScript backend project with package.json, tsconfig, and directory structure
steps: 1. Create root-level package.json with name 'dreamjob-backend', scripts (dev, build, start, seed), and TypeScript/Fastify dependencies
2. Create tsconfig.json targeting ES2022, module NodeNext, strict mode, outDir dist/
3. Create src/ directory with subdirectories: routes/, services/, services/ai/
4. Create .env.example with OPENAI_API_KEY and PORT=3000
5. Verify: npm install completes without errors

description: Install all required dependencies: Fastify, TypeBox, pdf-parse, @fastify/multipart, OpenAI SDK, tsx
steps: 1. Install production deps: fastify, @sinclair/typebox, pdf-parse, @fastify/multipart, @fastify/cors, openai
2. Install dev deps: typescript, tsx, @types/node
3. Verify: all dependencies resolve and tsc --noEmit passes on an empty project

description: Create the data/ directory structure with .gitkeep files and add data/ to .gitignore
steps: 1. Create data/ directory and data/uploads/ subdirectory
2. Add data/ to .gitignore (except .gitkeep if needed)
3. Ensure data/ is auto-created on first run if missing

description: Create Fastify app entry point (src/server.ts) with plugin registration and startup
steps: 1. Create src/server.ts that initializes Fastify with logger enabled
2. Register @fastify/multipart plugin for file uploads
3. Register all route plugins under /api prefix
4. Add CORS support for extension-ui origin (localhost:5173)
5. Listen on PORT from env (default 3000)
6. Verify: npm run dev starts server, GET / returns 404 or root response

description: Configure npm scripts: dev (tsx watch), build (tsc), start (node dist/server.js), seed
steps: 1. Add 'dev' script: tsx watch src/server.ts
2. Add 'build' script: tsc
3. Add 'start' script: node dist/server.js
4. Add 'seed' script: tsx src/seed.ts
5. Verify: npm run dev starts with hot reload, npm run build compiles to dist/

description: Implement JSON file store service (src/services/store.ts) for reading/writing data files
steps: 1. Create src/services/store.ts with generic read/write functions over JSON files using fs/promises
2. Implement readJSON<T>(filePath): reads and parses a JSON file, returns null if not found
3. Implement writeJSON<T>(filePath, data): writes data as formatted JSON to file
4. Implement readCollection<T>(filePath): reads a JSON array file, returns [] if not found
5. Implement writeCollection<T>(filePath, data): writes an array as JSON to file
6. Auto-create data/ and data/uploads/ directories if they don't exist on first write
7. Verify: unit test or manual test that read/write round-trips data correctly

description: Define file path constants for all data files
steps: 1. Define constants for all data file paths: profile.json, resume-upload.json, extraction.json, jobs-raw.json, jobs.json, cvs.json, ats-reviews.json, recruiter-reviews.json, review-agreements.json
2. Define uploads directory path constant (data/uploads/)
3. Export all path constants from store.ts or a dedicated paths module

description: Define TypeBox schemas for Profile core identity types (Identity, Constraints, targetRoles, professionalSummaryMaster)
steps: 1. Define Identity schema: name, headline, email, phone, location, links (linkedin, portfolio, github)
2. Define Constraints schema: preferredCvLanguage, maxCvPages, mustNotClaim array
3. Define targetRoles (string array) and professionalSummaryMaster (string) fields
4. Verify: schemas produce correct JSON Schema output

description: Define TypeBox schema for Experience with achievements and skillsUsed
steps: 1. Define Achievement sub-schema: text, metric, proofLevel
2. Define Experience schema: experienceId, title, company, location, startDate, endDate, description, achievements array, skillsUsed array
3. Verify: schema validates sample experience data

description: Define TypeBox schemas for supporting Profile types (Education, Skill, Certification, Language, Project, Reference)
steps: 1. Define Education schema: school, degree, field, year
2. Define Skill schema: name, category, level, years, evidenceRefs array
3. Define Certification schema: name, issuer, date
4. Define Language schema: name, level
5. Define Project schema: name, description, url, technologies array
6. Define Reference schema: name, title, company, email, phone, relationship
7. Verify: schemas validate sample data

description: Compose full Profile schema wrapping all sub-schemas with id, data, createdAt, updatedAt
steps: 1. Compose Profile data object containing: identity, targetRoles, professionalSummaryMaster, experiences, education, skills, certifications, languages, projects, references, constraints
2. Wrap in full Profile schema with id, data, createdAt, updatedAt
3. Export static TypeScript type
4. Verify: full Profile schema validates complete sample profile

description: Define TypeBox schemas and TypeScript types for ResumeUpload model
steps: 1. Define ResumeUpload schema: id, originalFilename, storagePath, uploadedAt, status (enum: uploaded/extracting/extracted/confirmed/failed), error (optional)
2. Export static TypeScript type from schema
3. Verify: schema validates sample data correctly

description: Define TypeBox schemas and TypeScript types for ExtractionResult model with confidence and review status
steps: 1. Define ConfidenceEntry schema: score (0.0-1.0), source (extracted/inferred/missing)
2. Define confidence map structure mirroring Profile shape with ConfidenceEntry leaves
3. Define ReviewStatus schema with per-section/item reviewed boolean tracking
4. Define completionStatus: markedComplete boolean, markedCompleteAt nullable datetime
5. Define full ExtractionResult schema: id, resumeUploadId, extractedAt, rawText, data (Profile shape), confidence, reviewStatus
6. Verify: schema validates sample extraction result

description: Define TypeBox schemas and TypeScript types for JobOfferRaw model
steps: 1. Define JobOfferRaw schema: id, source, sourceUrl, capturedAt, htmlSnapshotRef (optional), rawText, rawFields object (title, company, location, employment_type, etc.)
2. Export static TypeScript type
3. Verify: schema validates sample raw job data

description: Define TypeBox schemas and TypeScript types for JobPost (normalized) model
steps: 1. Define JobPost schema with all fields: id, jobOfferRawId, title, company, description, url, salary (optional), location, remoteMode (enum: onsite/hybrid/remote), employmentType (enum: full_time/part_time/contract/internship), seniority (enum: entry/mid/senior/lead/executive), jobSummary (string, not array)
2. Add array fields: responsibilities, requirementsMustHave, requirementsNiceToHave, keywords, tools, languages
3. Add optional fields: yearsExperienceMin, postedDate
4. Export static TypeScript type
5. Verify: schema validates sample normalized job post

description: Define TypeBox schemas and TypeScript types for GeneratedCV model
steps: 1. Define GeneratedCV schema: id, profileId, jobPostId, version, language, title
2. Add header object: fullName, headline, contact, links
3. Add summary string, skillsHighlighted array, experiencesSelected array (experienceId, rewrittenBullets[]), educationSelected array, certificationsSelected array
4. Add keywordsCovered, omittedItems, generationNotes arrays
5. Export static TypeScript type

description: Define TypeBox schemas and TypeScript types for ATSReview model
steps: 1. Define ATSReview schema: id, cvId, jobPostId, score (0-100), passed boolean
2. Add hardFiltersStatus array (filter, status, evidence objects), matchedKeywords, missingKeywords, formatFlags, recommendations arrays
3. Export static TypeScript type

description: Define TypeBox schemas and TypeScript types for RecruiterReview model
steps: 1. Define RecruiterReview schema: id, cvId, jobPostId, score (0-100), passed boolean
2. Add sub-scores: readabilityScore, credibilityScore, coherenceScore, evidenceScore (all 0-100)
3. Add strengths, concerns, recommendations arrays
4. Export static TypeScript type

description: Define TypeBox schemas and TypeScript types for ReviewAgreement model
steps: 1. Define ReviewAgreement schema: id, jobPostId, cvId, cvGenerationOk, atsOk, recruiterOk, reviewAgreementOk booleans
2. Add finalStatus enum: FINAL_APPROVED / REJECTED / NEEDS_REVISION
3. Add rejectionReasons array, iterationCount integer
4. Export static TypeScript type

description: Implement GET /api/profile endpoint to retrieve the full profile document
steps: 1. Create src/routes/profile.ts as a Fastify plugin
2. Implement GET /api/profile handler: read data/profile.json via store service
3. Return 200 with profile data, or 404 if no profile exists
4. Verify: curl GET /api/profile returns profile data after seeding

description: Implement PUT /api/profile endpoint to replace the full profile document
steps: 1. Define request body JSON Schema using TypeBox Profile schema
2. Implement PUT /api/profile handler: validate body, write to data/profile.json via store service
3. Set updatedAt to current ISO-8601 timestamp
4. Return 200 with updated profile
5. Verify: curl PUT /api/profile with valid JSON updates the file, invalid JSON returns 400

description: Implement POST /api/resume/upload file handling: accept multipart, validate PDF and size, save to uploads/
steps: 1. Create src/routes/resume.ts as a Fastify plugin
2. Implement POST /api/resume/upload: accept multipart/form-data with single 'file' field
3. Validate file is PDF (check mimetype), reject non-PDF with 400
4. Validate file size <= 10MB, reject oversized files with 400
5. Save file to data/uploads/resume.pdf (overwrite if exists)
6. Verify: upload a PDF via curl, check file saved to data/uploads/

description: Implement ResumeUpload document management: create/update resume-upload.json with status tracking
steps: 1. Create/update ResumeUpload document in data/resume-upload.json with status 'uploaded'
2. Set id, originalFilename, storagePath, uploadedAt fields
3. Verify: after upload, resume-upload.json contains correct document with status 'uploaded'

description: Implement extraction pipeline trigger and response assembly in POST /api/resume/upload
steps: 1. After file save and document creation, trigger extraction pipeline synchronously
2. On success: return { id, status, extractedData }
3. On failure: update resume-upload.json status to 'failed' with error, return error response
4. Verify: upload a PDF, confirm extraction triggers and response includes extracted data

description: Implement GET /api/resume/status endpoint
steps: 1. Implement GET /api/resume/status: read data/resume-upload.json via store
2. Return 200 with ResumeUpload document (status, timestamps, error if any)
3. Return 404 if no resume has been uploaded
4. Verify: returns correct status after upload, shows error if extraction failed

description: Implement GET /api/resume/extraction endpoint
steps: 1. Implement GET /api/resume/extraction: read data/extraction.json via store
2. Return 200 with full ExtractionResult (extracted data, confidence map, review status)
3. Return 404 if no extraction exists
4. Verify: returns extraction data after successful upload+extraction

description: Implement POST /api/resume/extraction/confirm endpoint
steps: 1. Implement POST /api/resume/extraction/confirm: read extraction.json, copy ExtractionResult.data into data/profile.json as the Profile
2. Update ResumeUpload status to 'confirmed'
3. Return 200 with the new Profile document
4. Return 404 if no extraction exists, 409 if already confirmed
5. Verify: confirming writes profile.json correctly, subsequent GET /api/profile returns extraction data

description: Implement PUT /api/resume/extraction/review endpoint for updating review status
steps: 1. Define request body schema: section (string, required), itemId (string, optional), reviewed (boolean, required)
2. Implement handler: read extraction.json, find matching section/item in reviewStatus, update reviewed flag
3. Handle scalar sections (identity) where itemId is omitted
4. Handle array sections (experiences, education, skills) where itemId identifies the item
5. Write updated extraction.json back
6. Return 200 with updated reviewStatus
7. Return 404 if no extraction exists, 400 if section/item not found
8. Verify: mark identity as reviewed, mark a specific experience as reviewed, check persistence

description: Implement GET /api/resume/completeness computed endpoint
steps: 1. Implement GET /api/resume/completeness: read extraction.json, compute progress/strength/checklist
2. Call completeness service to compute all values
3. Return { progress, strengthScore, missingSections, unresolvedSections, checklist, canMarkComplete }
4. Return 404 if no extraction exists
5. Verify: returns accurate progress percentage, canMarkComplete is false until all sections reviewed

description: Implement PDF text extraction using pdf-parse (src/services/extraction.ts)
steps: 1. Create src/services/extraction.ts
2. Implement extractTextFromPDF(filePath): read PDF file, use pdf-parse to extract plain text
3. Return raw text string
4. Handle errors (corrupt PDF, empty PDF) gracefully with descriptive error messages
5. Verify: extract text from a sample PDF, confirm output contains expected content

description: Implement AI structured extraction via OpenAI (extractProfileFromText)
steps: 1. Create function extractProfileFromText(text: string) in src/services/ai/openai.ts
2. Construct prompt instructing the model to extract data into exact Profile JSON shape
3. Include instructions for parallel confidence object with 0.0-1.0 scores and source labels
4. Use OpenAI structured output / JSON mode for reliable parsing
5. Return { data: ProfileDraft, confidence: ConfidenceMap }
6. Handle API errors, return meaningful error messages
7. Verify: send sample resume text, confirm output matches Profile schema with confidence scores

description: Implement extraction post-processing: ID assignment, review status init, date normalization
steps: 1. Assign experienceId values (exp_01, exp_02, ...) to extracted experiences
2. Initialize all reviewStatus entries to reviewed: false
3. Normalize date formats to ISO partial format (YYYY-MM)
4. Write data/extraction.json with full ExtractionResult
5. Update data/resume-upload.json status to 'extracted'
6. On failure, set resume-upload status to 'failed' with error message
7. Verify: post-processed extraction has valid IDs, all review statuses are false

description: Implement completeness scoring pure function (src/services/completeness.ts)
steps: 1. Create src/services/completeness.ts
2. Implement progress calculation: reviewed items / total items * 100

3. Implement strength score with exact point allocation: identity (15), 1+ exp (15), 2+ exp (25), exp with 2+ achievements (5 each, max 15), 1+ education (10), 5+ skills (10), 10+ skills (15), certifications (5), projects (5), languages (5), summary (5)
4. Implement missingSections: list sections with no data
5. Implement unresolvedSections: list sections with unreviewed items
6. Build checklist array with label/met pairs
7. Implement canMarkComplete logic: identity reviewed AND all experiences reviewed AND all education reviewed AND all skills reviewed
8. Verify: test with empty profile (0 progress, low strength), test with complete profile (100 progress, high strength)

description: Implement POST /api/jobs/raw endpoint for extension to submit scraped job data
steps: 1. Create src/routes/jobs.ts as a Fastify plugin
2. Define request body schema matching JobOfferRaw rawFields shape
3. Implement POST /api/jobs/raw: validate body, assign auto-generated id (raw_1, raw_2...), set capturedAt
4. Append to data/jobs-raw.json collection
5. Auto-trigger normalization into a JobPost (call normalize service)
6. Return 201 with created JobOfferRaw and normalized JobPost
7. Verify: POST raw job data, confirm both raw and normalized entries created

description: Implement GET /api/jobs/raw and GET /api/jobs/raw/:id endpoints
steps: 1. Implement GET /api/jobs/raw: read data/jobs-raw.json, return full array
2. Implement GET /api/jobs/raw/:id: find by id in collection, return 200 or 404
3. Define :id param schema (non-empty string)
4. Verify: list returns all raw captures, get by id returns correct one

description: Implement GET /api/jobs and GET /api/jobs/:id endpoints for normalized job posts
steps: 1. Implement GET /api/jobs: read data/jobs.json, return full array
2. Implement GET /api/jobs/:id: find by id, return 200 or 404
3. Verify: list returns normalized jobs, get by id returns correct one

description: Implement PUT /api/jobs/:id endpoint to update a normalized job post
steps: 1. Define request body schema using TypeBox JobPost schema
2. Implement PUT /api/jobs/:id: find by id, merge updates, write back to collection
3. Set updatedAt timestamp
4. Return 200 with updated job post, or 404 if not found
5. Verify: update a job post field, confirm persistence

description: Implement DELETE /api/jobs/:id endpoint
steps: 1. Implement DELETE /api/jobs/:id: find by id, remove from collection, write back
2. Return 204 on success, 404 if not found
3. Verify: delete a job, confirm it no longer appears in GET /api/jobs

description: Implement job normalization service (src/services/normalize.ts) using OpenAI
steps: 1. Create src/services/normalize.ts
2. Implement normalizeJobOffer(raw: JobOfferRaw): Promise<JobPost>
3. Send raw text and fields to OpenAI with prompt to extract structured JobPost fields
4. Map response to JobPost schema: title, company, description, location, remoteMode, employmentType, seniority, responsibilities, requirements, keywords, tools, languages
5. Assign auto-generated id (job_1, job_2...) and link jobOfferRawId
6. Append normalized job to data/jobs.json
7. Verify: normalize a raw LinkedIn job capture, confirm all fields populated correctly

description: Implement POST /api/cvs/generate endpoint to kick off the full agent pipeline
steps: 1. Create src/routes/cvs.ts as a Fastify plugin
2. Define request body schema: { jobPostId: string, language: string }
3. Implement handler: load Profile and JobPost by IDs
4. Return 404 if profile or job post not found
5. Call cv-generator orchestrator service with profile + job post + language
6. Store GeneratedCV, ATSReview, RecruiterReview, ReviewAgreement results
7. Return 200 with generated CV and review results
8. Verify: generate a CV for a seeded profile and job, confirm all artifacts stored

description: Implement GET /api/cvs and GET /api/cvs/:id endpoints
steps: 1. Implement GET /api/cvs: read data/cvs.json, return full array
2. Implement GET /api/cvs/:id: find by id, return 200 or 404
3. Verify: list returns generated CVs, get by id returns correct one

description: Implement DELETE /api/cvs/:id endpoint
steps: 1. Implement DELETE /api/cvs/:id: remove CV from data/cvs.json
2. Also clean up associated ats-reviews, recruiter-reviews, review-agreements
3. Return 204 on success, 404 if not found
4. Verify: delete a CV, confirm associated reviews also removed

description: Implement GET /api/cvs/:id/ats-review endpoint
steps: 1. Implement GET /api/cvs/:id/ats-review: find ATS review by cvId in data/ats-reviews.json
2. Return 200 with ATSReview, or 404 if not found
3. Verify: returns correct ATS review for a generated CV

description: Implement GET /api/cvs/:id/recruiter-review endpoint
steps: 1. Implement GET /api/cvs/:id/recruiter-review: find RecruiterReview by cvId in data/recruiter-reviews.json
2. Return 200 with RecruiterReview, or 404 if not found
3. Verify: returns correct recruiter review for a generated CV

description: Set up OpenAI SDK client (src/services/ai/openai.ts) with API key configuration
steps: 1. Create src/services/ai/openai.ts
2. Initialize OpenAI client using OPENAI_API_KEY from environment
3. Export client instance and helper functions for chat completions
4. Add error handling for missing API key (throw descriptive error at startup)
5. Verify: client initializes without error when API key is set

description: Implement core generateTargetedCV: prompt construction and structured output returning GeneratedCV shape
steps: 1. Implement generateTargetedCV function in a dedicated agent file (src/services/ai/candidate-agent.ts)
2. Accept input: job_offer (JobPost), candidate_master_profile (Profile), generation_rules (language, max_pages, tone, truthfulness_mode)
3. Construct prompt enforcing business rules: never invent experience/skills, prioritize evidence-backed items, optimize for target job
4. Use OpenAI structured output to return GeneratedCV shape
5. Verify: generates valid CV structure from sample profile+job

description: Implement coverage map: match job requirements to profile evidence refs and list uncovered requirements
steps: 1. Extend generateTargetedCV output to include coverage_map
2. coverage_map.matched_requirements: array of { requirement, evidence_ref } linking job requirements to profile items
3. coverage_map.uncovered_requirements: array of requirements not addressed by the CV
4. Verify: coverage map accurately reflects which job requirements are covered

description: Implement self-check: detect unsupported claims and generate warnings
steps: 1. Extend generateTargetedCV output to include self_check
2. self_check.unsupported_claims_found: boolean flag
3. self_check.warnings: array of warning strings for any claims not backed by profile evidence
4. Verify: self-check flags when CV contains claims not in the master profile

description: Implement revision support: accept previous ATS/Recruiter reviews as revision_context
steps: 1. Add optional revision_context parameter: { previous_ats_review, previous_recruiter_review }
2. When revision_context is provided, include review feedback in the prompt for the agent to address
3. Ensure revised CV addresses specific blocking issues and missing keywords from prior reviews
4. Verify: passing a failing ATS review as context produces a revised CV that addresses the feedback

description: Implement ATS Agent: scores CV for machine/ATS compatibility
steps: 1. Implement ATS Agent function accepting job_offer (JobPost), generated_cv (GeneratedCV), scoring_rules
2. Default scoring_rules: passing_score 75, weight_keywords 0.5, weight_hard_filters 0.3, weight_structure 0.2
3. Construct prompt for ATS evaluation: keyword presence, hard filter coverage, clear job titles, no vague wording
4. Return ATSReview with score (0-100), passed boolean, hardFiltersStatus array, matchedKeywords, missingKeywords, formatFlags, recommendations
5. Return decision: status (pass/fail) and blocking_issues array
6. Score formula: 50% keywords + 30% hard filters + 20% structure
7. Thresholds: 0-49 low, 50-74 partial, 75-100 pass
8. Verify: review a sample CV against a job, confirm score and keyword analysis are reasonable

description: Implement Recruiter Agent: evaluates CV credibility, readability, and persuasiveness
steps: 1. Implement Recruiter Agent function accepting job_offer (JobPost), generated_cv (GeneratedCV), review_rules
2. Default review_rules: passing_score 75, weight_readability 0.25, weight_credibility 0.35, weight_evidence 0.2, weight_coherence 0.2
3. Construct prompt for recruiter evaluation: readability, career-to-role coherence, concrete proof, no over-promising, density vs clarity
4. Return RecruiterReview with score (0-100), passed boolean, sub-scores (readability, credibility, coherence, evidence)
5. Return strengths, concerns, recommendations arrays
6. Return decision: status (pass/fail) and blocking_issues array
7. Score formula: 35% credibility + 25% readability + 20% coherence + 20% evidence
8. Thresholds: 0-49 low credibility, 50-74 credible but weak, 75-100 pass
9. Verify: review a sample CV, confirm sub-scores and feedback are meaningful

description: Implement single-pass orchestrator: call Candidate → ATS → Recruiter agents sequentially, store artifacts
steps: 1. Create src/services/cv-generator.ts
2. Implement orchestrate(profile, jobPost, language) function
3. Call Candidate Agent to generate initial CV
4. Call ATS Agent to review the generated CV
5. Call Recruiter Agent to review the generated CV
6. Store all artifacts: GeneratedCV in cvs.json, ATSReview in ats-reviews.json, RecruiterReview in recruiter-reviews.json
7. Verify: single-pass pipeline runs end-to-end with sample data, all artifacts stored

description: Implement decision evaluation: pass/fail logic based on agent results
steps: 1. After all agents run, evaluate: cvGenerationOk (CV produced), atsOk (ATS passed), recruiterOk (Recruiter passed)
2. FINAL_APPROVED when all three are true
3. Otherwise mark as needing revision or rejection
4. Verify: passing scores produce FINAL_APPROVED, any failure prevents approval

description: Implement revision loop: retry with feedback when ATS or Recruiter fails (max 2 iterations)
steps: 1. If ATS fails: feed ATS review back to Candidate Agent as revision_context
2. If Recruiter fails: feed Recruiter review back to Candidate Agent as revision_context
3. If both fail: consolidate both reviews into single feedback for Candidate Agent
4. Track iteration count, maximum 2 revision iterations
5. After 2 failures: end with REJECTED_FOR_REVIEW status
6. Verify: simulate a failing ATS score, confirm revision triggers and re-evaluates

description: Implement ReviewAgreement construction: build and store final decision document
steps: 1. Build ReviewAgreement with: id, jobPostId, cvId, cvGenerationOk, atsOk, recruiterOk, reviewAgreementOk
2. Set finalStatus: FINAL_APPROVED, REJECTED, or NEEDS_REVISION
3. Populate rejectionReasons array from blocking issues
4. Set iterationCount from the orchestrator loop counter
5. Store in data/review-agreements.json
6. Verify: approved pipeline stores ReviewAgreement with FINAL_APPROVED, rejected stores with reasons

description: Implement AddonResult construction for returning results to the extension
steps: 1. Map FINAL_APPROVED to status 'accepted', REJECTED_FOR_REVIEW to 'rejected'
2. Compute overall_score from ATS and Recruiter scores
3. Include scores object: { ats_score, recruiter_score }
4. Aggregate strengths, weaknesses, recommendations from both reviews
5. Return complete AddonResult regardless of outcome
6. Verify: accepted result has correct scores and aggregated feedback, rejected result includes rejection reasons

description: Add shared enum and constraint definitions for reuse across route validation schemas
steps: 1. Define enum schemas: employmentType, seniority, remoteMode, level, finalStatus
2. Define shared maxLength constraints (name <= 200, description <= 10000)
3. Define shared :id param schema (non-empty string) for all parameterized routes
4. Verify: shared schemas export correctly and can be referenced by route files

description: Add request validation schema to Profile routes (PUT body)
steps: 1. Add body schema to PUT /api/profile with required fields (identity.name, identity.email) and string limits
2. Verify: invalid PUT /api/profile returns 400 with descriptive error

description: Add request validation schemas to Resume routes (upload multipart, review body, params)
steps: 1. Add body schema to POST /api/resume/upload (multipart validation)
2. Add body schema to PUT /api/resume/extraction/review (section required, itemId optional, reviewed required)
3. Verify: invalid resume requests return 400 with descriptive errors

description: Add request validation schemas to Jobs routes (POST raw body, PUT body, :id params)
steps: 1. Add body schema to POST /api/jobs/raw with rawText required and rawFields object
2. Add body schema to PUT /api/jobs/:id using JobPost schema with enum validation
3. Add params schema to /api/jobs/raw/:id and /api/jobs/:id routes
4. Verify: invalid jobs requests return 400 with descriptive errors

description: Add request validation schemas to CVs routes (POST generate body, :id params)
steps: 1. Add body schema to POST /api/cvs/generate (jobPostId required, language required)
2. Add params schema to /api/cvs/:id, /api/cvs/:id/ats-review, /api/cvs/:id/recruiter-review
3. Verify: invalid CVs requests return 400 with descriptive errors

description: Differentiate extraction error types and return appropriate HTTP status codes
steps: 1. On PDF parse failure (corrupt/empty PDF): return 400 Bad Request
2. On AI extraction failure (OpenAI error): return 500 with OpenAI error message
3. On post-processing failure: return 500 with descriptive error
4. Ensure each error type stores a distinct error message in resume-upload.json (basic failure path handled by pipeline trigger task)
5. Verify: upload a corrupt file returns 400, simulate AI failure returns 500, both store distinct error messages

description: Handle missing OpenAI API key and AI service errors gracefully
steps: 1. Check for OPENAI_API_KEY at startup, log warning if missing
2. Return 503 with descriptive message when AI endpoints are called without API key
3. Handle OpenAI rate limits and transient errors with descriptive error responses
4. Verify: start server without API key, confirm AI endpoints return 503 not crash

description: Ensure CORS is configured for the Chrome extension to communicate with the backend
steps: 1. Verify @fastify/cors is registered in server.ts (installed in scaffolding, registered in server setup)
2. Verify origin allows chrome-extension:// and localhost:5173 (Vite dev server)
3. Verify allowed methods: GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, OPTIONS
4. Verify allowed headers: Content-Type, Authorization
5. Verify: extension can make API calls to backend without CORS errors

description: End-to-end flow: upload resume -> extract -> review -> confirm -> generate CV
steps: 1. Seed demo profile via npm run seed
2. Upload a sample PDF resume via POST /api/resume/upload
3. Check extraction result via GET /api/resume/extraction
4. Review all sections via PUT /api/resume/extraction/review
5. Confirm extraction via POST /api/resume/extraction/confirm
6. Submit a raw job offer via POST /api/jobs/raw
7. Generate a CV via POST /api/cvs/generate with the job post ID
8. Retrieve generated CV and reviews via GET endpoints
9. Verify: all data flows through correctly, all JSON files are populated
